mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above 600 thousand units signals a durable recovery in residential construction and downstream consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months where HOUST exceeds 600.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in industrial production and a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent generate an interaction effect where easy monetary policy amplifies the equity market response to positive supply signals, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO growth and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% does not produce significantly higher average 20-day forward returns than INDPRO growth alone (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During the post-shock recovery phase, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals reflating aggregate demand and reduced deflation risk, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations during post-crisis recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals a discrete improvement in labor market tightness that translates into higher expected household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of UNRATE declining by more than 0.2pp in a month are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 40% of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and real retail sales confirm a coordinated recovery in labor income and consumer spending, generating a non-linear positive effect on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceeds the sum of individual series effects.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS and RSAFS both increasing does not produce a significantly higher average 20-day return than either increasing alone (interaction term p-value > 0.05 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real chained GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that underpins corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals a rapid snap-back in manufacturing utilization and inventory rebuilding that outperforms sluggish expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly increases greater than 1% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.
